集训3 · 假期复习任务卡 · Day 20 of 24

The Injured Seagull

📖 Reading

286 words · 27 target words · listen while you read

Tier 1（12词） Tier 2（6词） Tier 3（9词）

Ben was an active, cheerful boy who loved Sports Day more than any other day of the school year. His favorite subject at school was P.E., and every afternoon after class, he practiced jumping along a sandy path near the beach, hoping to achieve a new personal best.

One afternoon, Ben kicked off his shoes and ran down the trail toward the sand with his loyal old dog beside him. He wanted to reach the flags at the far end before dinner. At the bottom of the hill, something small and gray caught his eye.

It was a little animal — a young seagull with a hurt wing. Ben seemed to freeze for a second, wondering what had happened to its wing. The bird could not stand on one leg, and it gave a loud, scared cry. His dog barked once, but Ben told him to be quiet.

Ben knelt down slowly and tried to communicate with the bird using soft, calm sounds. He did not expect it to trust him so fast, but after a few minutes, the bird let him gently lift it into his jacket. He carried it home and helped his mother find a box for it.

The next morning, a letter came in the mail from the local rescue center, with a similar story about a rescued fox. Ben's mother had called them the night before. The volunteers came, checked the seagull, and said it would be free to fly again soon.

At the fitness test that Friday, Ben ran and jumped better than ever. It felt amazing to know the answer to his own question: helping others, even in small ways, was the real key to feeling proud of himself.

🔍 Reading Comprehension

5 questions · tap to check your answer

1In "Ben seemed to freeze for a second, wondering what had happened to its wing," what does freeze mean here?
B — Ben stopped moving for a moment because he was surprised and did not know what to do.
2In "Ben knelt down slowly and tried to communicate with the bird using soft, calm sounds," what does communicate mean?
B — Ben used soft sounds to try to share calm feelings with the bird, which is a way of communicating.
3In "He carried it home and helped his mother find a box for it," what does find mean?
B — Ben and his mother needed to locate a box to keep the hurt bird safe.
4Why did Ben stop running on his way to the flags?
C — something small and gray caught his eye, and it turned out to be a seagull with a hurt wing.
5How did Ben feel at the end of the story, and why?
A — Ben did well at the fitness test and felt amazing, realizing that helping others in small ways made him proud of himself.
🔁
